architectureStandards.name: Knowledge and Universal Intake Runtime v1
architectureStandards.records: Authorized local intake boundary with content-addressed preservation, deterministic extraction, opaque media/archive preservation, SQLite knowledge records, provenance, candidate status, offline proofs, and bounded lexical retrieval.

architectureStandards.name: Capability Engine and Recursive Learning v1
architectureStandards.records: Governed recursive learning and capability version lifecycle with authorized sessions, immutable candidate bundles, isolated execution, deterministic evaluation, baseline comparison, exact certification, Control Plane promotion, active versions, regression evidence, rollback, and repeatable offline proof.

architectureStandards.name: Studio Runtime v1
architectureStandards.records: Studio registry, exact version digests, sessions, stage transitions, artifact lineage, review state, immutable package evidence, candidate learning-signal routing, and repeatable offline proofs.

architectureStandards.name: Integrated Offline Loop v1
architectureStandards.records: Required learning preflight before capability or Studio selection, bounded known-record classification, certified-alternative selection, governed-override evidence, offline service composition, final package evidence, and repeatable model-free proof.

architectureStandards.name: Evidence Studio v1
architectureStandards.records: First certified Studio definition for source-grounded professional briefs with claim ledger, source map, deterministic evaluation, operator review, final package, and candidate-only learning signals.

architectureStandards.name: Local Model Runtime v1
architectureStandards.records: Provider-independent local model invocation boundary with deterministic fixture provider, disabled real-local adapter metadata, explicit authorization, candidate-intelligence-only output, SQLite invocation records, and offline proof.

architectureStandards.name: Evaluation Engine v1
architectureStandards.records: Deterministic evaluation of immutable execution evidence with assertion outcomes, aggregation, SQLite persistence, and Control Plane gate evidence.

architectureStandards.name: Intelligence Pipeline v1
architectureStandards.records: Intent to closeout lifecycle for governed Capabilities and Applications.

architectureStandards.name: Intelligence Promotion Model v1
architectureStandards.records: Evidence, Knowledge, Understanding, and Strategy promotion terms.

architectureStandards.name: Architecture Freeze v1
architectureStandards.records: Base MVP architecture freeze, allowed changes, prohibited changes, and emergency ADR process.

architectureStandards.name: Learning Versus Innovation Governance v1
architectureStandards.records: Learning improves existing capabilities; innovation creates materially new candidates under stronger governance.

architectureStandards.name: Evidence-Driven Recurrence Prevention and Governed Innovation v1
architectureStandards.records: Cross-cutting Base MVP acceptance invariant for evidence-linked learning signals, recurrence prevention, scoped lesson retrieval, governed overrides, lesson supersession, and distinct governed innovation. Milestone 14 bounded active lesson, prevention, override, supersession, improvement, and innovation proof is certified.

architectureStandards.name: S.E.R.A. Personal and Professional V1 Roadmap
architectureStandards.records: Post-Base MVP planning lock for Milestones 17 through 24. It preserves Base MVP completion at Milestone 16 and does not implement additional Milestone 13 behavior, web, browser, iOS Runtime, update, backup, enterprise, Hive Mode, robotics, or post-Base Runtime behavior.

architectureStandards.name: Time-to-Certified-Capability v1
architectureStandards.records: North-star metric and guardrails for safe capability growth.

seedClassifications.migrationTarget: permanent runtime package or consolidation source
production module, evaluation asset, failure fixture, or legacy

targetSubsystems.notes: Local Runtime Host v1 starts Runtime Services in deterministic dependency order, preserves installation identity, creates per-start runtime instance identity, reports health, writes lifecycle evidence, and shuts down cleanly. SQLite Operational State and Persistent Runtime Recovery are separate required Runtime Services.
Permanent contract, identity, safety, lifecycle, and terminal-state authority. Current package placement is provisional until upward dependencies are removed.

limitations: reference executor does not run shell commands
does not perform Git merge, push, tag, reset, stash, or branch actions
does not begin SQLite Runtime State
owner approvals are explicit spec fields in v1
legacy phase, browser, OneDrive, and ZIP workflows remain evidence only
targetSubsystems.notes: Local attempt, stage, gate, evidence, terminal-decision, verification, and closeout authority. Replaces phase-numbered orchestration as production authority for terminal state, while preserving legacy workflows as evidence only.

limitations: does not classify architecture or legacy status
does not infer callers or semantic dependency graphs
Git metadata is optional and local-only
test ownership is heuristic and must include confidence and evidence
targetSubsystems.nextGate: Repository Snapshot v1 certification and actual-repository proof
targetSubsystems.notes: Deterministic bounded inventory of repository source, runtime data, generated artifacts, and legacy surfaces. Must precede Repository Truth. Mark as certified only after the full validation and certification suite passes.

limitations: consumes Repository Snapshot evidence and does not duplicate scanning
does not claim semantic import/caller authority
classification is deterministic and confidence-scored
does not remediate legacy or generated assets
targetSubsystems.notes: Classifies snapshot evidence into components, declared dependency graph, test ownership, findings, inventory reconciliation, and legacy authority risks without relying on heuristic certainty.

limitations: persistent recovery is implemented by runtime-recovery
does not implement distributed coordination
does not use HTTP, network listeners, cloud services, or model calls
does not replace Unified Control Plane terminal-decision authority
targetSubsystems.notes: SQLite-backed local operational state for command idempotency, attempts, transitions, gate outcomes, evidence references, leases, backups, deterministic exports, and integrity checks. Existing JSON/JSONL stores remain useful evidence but should converge behind one Runtime-owned state boundary over time.

limitations: does not implement isolated execution
does not execute arbitrary subprocess workloads
does not implement distributed recovery, Hive Mode, or remote workers
does not claim exactly-once arbitrary side effects
targetSubsystems.notes: Bounded local recovery coordinator for durable nonterminal attempts. It resumes only restart-safe checkpoints, creates linked retries at clear failed boundaries, blocks uncertain side effects for review, and writes recovery evidence under Runtime Host health.

limitations: not a hostile-code sandbox
does not expose unrestricted shell execution
does not accept arbitrary executable paths
does not implement containers, virtual machines, network namespaces, distributed execution, or Evaluation Engine behavior
targetSubsystems.notes: Governed local execution chamber for explicitly authorized workloads. It creates temporary workspaces outside the repository, launches approved executable adapters with shell disabled, enforces output/timeout/cancellation limits, writes SQLite execution records, harvests declared outputs, cleans up, and preserves Control Plane terminal authority.
Unified deterministic and rubric-based evaluation. Existing certs, phase verifiers, and tests require classification before consolidation.

limitations: does not load arbitrary code
does not execute unrestricted shell
does not invoke real models or public network
does not mutate core source automatically
does not implement Desktop Operator, Studio, distributed capability, or Hive Mode
targetSubsystems.notes: Capability Engine and Recursive Learning v1 creates authorized learning sessions, immutable candidate bundles, deterministic evaluation and comparison evidence, exact certification records, explicit Control Plane promotion, active-version state, and rollback evidence.

limitations: does not download, install, or invoke real models
does not add cloud providers or credentials
does not permit model-driven tool execution or source mutation
model output remains candidate intelligence only
the legacy packages/model-provider surface is compatibility evidence, not Runtime authority
targetSubsystems.notes: Provider-independent local model Runtime boundary. It registers deterministic fixture and disabled real-local providers, enforces explicit authorization and offline/local policy, records model invocations in SQLite schema v5, writes redacted evidence, and integrates with Runtime Host health.

limitations: does not use OCR or transcription providers
does not fetch public URLs
does not extract archives
does not execute imported content
does not promote candidate knowledge to truth
targetSubsystems.nextGate: Capability Genome and Learning Engine v1
targetSubsystems.notes: Normalizes authorized local text, files, directories, predownloaded snapshots, URL references without fetching, generated fixtures, opaque media, and archives into candidate knowledge records with deterministic evidence and lexical retrieval.
Loopback-only gateway for Desktop Operator sessions, approvals, notifications, events, safe evidence viewing, and operator audit records.
Local graphical operator surface with integrity-checked local assets and no remote references.

targetSubsystems.name: Learning Generalization, Recurrence Prevention, and Innovation Proof v1
limitations: bounded deterministic proof scope
no automatic lesson activation
no real model invocation
no public network use
clean-machine restart remains Milestone 15
targetSubsystems.notes: Canonical Milestone 14 capability. Learning Governance Runtime owns durable lesson, prevention, override, improvement, and innovation evidence below Operator Gateway; Integrated Loop consumes its read-only durable preflight API.

limitations: bounded deterministic proof scope
no automatic lesson activation
no real model invocation
no public network use
targetSubsystems.notes: Milestone 14 certifies evidence-linked failure, correction, regression, override, and improvement signal recording in the Learning Governance Runtime.

limitations: candidate management is not the same as active lesson behavior
one observation is not automatically a certified lesson
model output is candidate intelligence only
targetSubsystems.notes: Milestone 14 implements the governed lifecycle for candidate, certified inactive, active, superseded, and rolled-back lessons with exact evidence references.

limitations: bounded deterministic proof scope
prevention behavior requires certified active lesson scope
out-of-scope contexts must not be incorrectly blocked
targetSubsystems.notes: Milestone 14 certifies active prevention-rule retrieval and known-failure behavior for exact, related, governed override, and out-of-scope contexts.

limitations: does not certify lessons
does not activate prevention rules
does not promote innovation
does not complete Milestone 14 recurrence-prevention proof
targetSubsystems.notes: Milestone 13 integrated preflight before materially similar work retrieves and classifies applicable lessons, known failures, prevention rules, certified alternatives, and active overrides before capability or Studio selection.

limitations: existing Capability Engine comparison foundations do not certify reusable lessons by themselves
targetSubsystems.notes: Milestone 14 records measurable baseline comparison evidence for improvement certification.

limitations: innovation cannot activate itself
no automatic innovation promotion exists
no later-milestone restart or portability behavior is implemented
targetSubsystems.notes: Milestone 14 certifies the distinct governed innovation lane with isolated experimentation, baseline comparison, certification-inactive state, explicit promotion boundary, and rollback readiness.
First reference MVP capability. Must accept a goal, ingest local evidence, identify missing capability, plan, execute in isolation, evaluate, learn, persist state, and run offline.

acceptanceCriteria: legacy browser and overlay transport isolated
